label: 3304 Acquisition of buildings and sites

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Administrator of General Services may acquire, by purchase, condemnation, donation, exchange, or otherwise, any building and its site which the Administrator decides is necessary to carry out the duties of the Administrator under this chapter.

children.heading: .—Acquisition of Land or Interest in Land for Use as Sites
children.label: b .—Acquisition of Land or Interest in Land for Use as Sites
children.text: The Administrator may acquire, by purchase, condemnation, donation, exchange, or otherwise, land or an interest in land the Administrator considers necessary for use as sites, or additions to sites, for public buildings authorized to be constructed or altered under this chapter.

children.heading: .—Public Buildings Used for Post Office Purposes
children.label: c .—Public Buildings Used for Post Office Purposes
children.text: When any part of a public building is to be used for post office purposes, the Administrator shall act jointly with the United States Postal Service in selecting the town or city where the building is to be constructed, and in selecting the site in the town or city for the building.

children.heading: .—Solicitation of Proposals for Sale, Donation, or Exchange of Real Property
children.label: d .—Solicitation of Proposals for Sale, Donation, or Exchange of Real Property

children.text: select the site that the Administrator believes is the most advantageous to the Government, all factors considered; and
acquire the site without regard to division C (except sections 3302, 3501(b), 3509, 3906, 4710, and 4711) of subtitle I of title 41.
